final:
One abstraction I’d delete: `VaultLibrary` in `emptyos/sdk/vault_library.py`.

It tries to be a universal CRUD/search layer for any vault-backed collection, but it leaks a lot of app-specific policy into a shared base class: tag selection, aliasing, fallback directory scans, sorting, type coercion, and file rewriting. That makes every app fit the same “note library” shape even when the domain doesn’t want it. If I had a blank slate, I’d keep only tiny vault helpers and let each app own its own query/write logic. The abstraction is convenient, but it’s also where a lot of hidden coupling accumulates.
